nits.issue: The boundary rule states `internal/store/globaldb` must not import `internal/network`, but the `ConversationStore` interface (defined in `internal/network`) embeds `ConversationRef` from `internal/network` in `ListConversationMessages`, forcing globaldb to import the package it is forbidden from importing.
nits.suggested_fix: Move `ConversationRef` (and its `Surface` alias) into `internal/store` — or expose primitives `(channel, surface, threadID, directID)` in the interface signature — so globaldb can implement the interface without importing `internal/network`.

nits.issue: Migration version `16` is hardcoded with the caveat 'unless another migration lands first', but no implementation guidance forces the agent to verify the next-free version at write time.
nits.suggested_fix: Add an explicit instruction that the implementation must read `globalSchemaMigrations` at task start and use whichever next sequential version is free, and that the checksum string must include that version.

nits.issue: `network_thread_participants` has `ON DELETE CASCADE` against `network_threads`, but the spec does not state that SQLite `PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON` is in effect for the global DB; without it the FK is unenforced.
nits.suggested_fix: Either confirm the existing globaldb already enables `foreign_keys` and reference that fact, or add an explicit pragma assertion to the migration plus a test.

nits.section: Implementation Design — Same-Transaction Write Strategy step 8
nits.issue: Spec mentions an 'allowed terminal duplicate' carve-out when rejecting messages on a terminal `network_work`, but never defines what makes a duplicate allowed.
nits.suggested_fix: Define the allowed terminal duplicate set explicitly (e.g., idempotent re-receipts with same `message_id`) or remove the carve-out and reject all post-terminal writes.

nits.section: Implementation Design — Direct Room Resolution Algorithm
nits.issue: Algorithm handles the `(channel, peer_a, peer_b)` UNIQUE collision but does not specify the behavior when `INSERT OR IGNORE` fails on the `(channel, direct_id)` PRIMARY KEY for a different pair (vanishingly rare hash collision) and the subsequent SELECT returns zero rows.
nits.suggested_fix: Document the zero-row-after-IGNORE case explicitly: return `ErrDirectRoomCollision` and add a unit test that simulates the contention.

nits.section: Implementation Design — Same-Transaction Write Strategy step 4
nits.issue: 'Verify the target container exists or create it when this is a valid thread-opening or direct-room-opening write' leaves 'valid' undefined — spec never says whether arbitrary first-send to a fresh `thread_id` auto-creates the thread or requires an explicit open verb.
nits.suggested_fix: Pin the rule: declare that the first conversation-bearing message with a new `thread_id` opens the thread, or add an explicit thread-open primitive. Define the `thread_id` grammar (charset, length, uniqueness scope).

nits.issue: Network hooks 'dispatch at the state-transition call site after durable commit' but commit-then-crash semantics (at-least-once vs at-most-once vs lost) are not stated; agents and extensions need to know whether to deduplicate.
nits.suggested_fix: State the delivery contract explicitly (best-effort fire-and-forget post-commit, no replay) and call it out in the hooks family docs.

nits.section: Implementation Design — Validators and Reason Codes
nits.issue: Validator list does not enumerate 'reject `thread_id` or `direct_id` without a corresponding `surface`'. The kind-rules table implies it for conversation-bearing kinds, but explicit symmetric rejection would tighten ingress.
nits.suggested_fix: Add explicit invariants and unit tests: any envelope with `thread_id`/`direct_id` set MUST set matching `surface`; any `surface` set MUST set matching container ID; surface+IDs MUST be absent on `gret`/`whois`.

nits.issue: `network_work` carries `(channel, surface, thread_id, direct_id)` but has no foreign keys to `network_threads`/`network_direct_rooms`; CHECK constraints alone do not prevent dangling work bound to a missing container.
nits.suggested_fix: Add per-surface FKs (or a constraint-trigger) ensuring `(channel, thread_id)` exists when `surface='thread'` and `(channel, direct_id)` exists when `surface='direct'`, with `ON DELETE RESTRICT`.

nits.issue: Correlation between `work_id` and any `task_runs` row triggered by a network message is described as 'trace_id, causation_id, and task metadata', but no concrete task-metadata field is named, so observability/triage cannot deterministically jump from work to its task run.
nits.suggested_fix: Pin a canonical metadata key (e.g., `task_runs.metadata.network_work_id`) and require the network ingress path to set it whenever a task run is created from a work-bearing message.

nits.section: API, CLI, Tooling — Web Route Map
nits.issue: Composer behavior at `/_app/network/$channel/threads` (channel level, no specific thread/direct selected) is undefined — open new thread? disabled? redirect?
nits.suggested_fix: Specify the channel-level composer affordance: either disable it, redirect to a thread-creation flow, or define the implicit new-thread payload.

nits.issue: Task 08 bundles unit/integration/web/E2E/real-scenario QA into a single task; CLAUDE.md mandates a `qa-report` + `qa-execution` pair appended by `cy-create-tasks` with explicit e2e for UI-bearing features.
nits.suggested_fix: Note explicitly in the MVP boundary that task generation will append the qa-report + qa-execution pair (with Playwright e2e for the new web routes), so the task-decomposition phase does not collapse them.

nits.issue: Envelope uses pointer-to-alias `*Surface` and pointer strings for nullable fields, but JCS canonicalization rule 'field absence is signed by omission; a receiver must not insert default values before verifying' is only stated in the RFC 004 section — not enforced by a marshal/unmarshal contract.
nits.suggested_fix: Add a unit-test requirement: round-trip an envelope through JSON and JCS and assert that absent vs zero-valued nullable fields produce different signed canonical bytes.

summary: All eight round-1 blockers are materially addressed: numbered migration with rebuild-and-drop semantics, deterministic direct-room resolver with race-safe transaction shape, durable `network_work` binding kept strictly out of the task queue, RFC 004 signed-field set extended, `task_runs` invariants preserved, hooks dispatched post-commit at the call site, and `claim_token` redaction reaffirmed across every surface. Remaining items are clarifications, observability gaps, and a minor boundary-ownership inconsistency around `ConversationRef` placement — none of which prevent execution; they should be cleaned up alongside implementation rather than block approval.
